title: Blower system for magnetic resonance imaging machines
abstract: This invention relates to magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) machines. Previously, there is a need for a system to cool a patient in an MRI machine. Embodiments of the present invention use a blower system (10) that is configured to blow an air source into the interior of the MRI machine (36). The blower system may include a generally cylindrical housing (11) to match the diameter of the head end of the MRI machine chamber (37). A fan (40) may pull ambient room temperature or cooled air from either an intake port (38) or from the oxygen lines (26, 28) to dynamically circulate the air through the housing (11) effectively immersing the patient in air which is adjustable by automatic control or by the patient.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS : 1. A blower system for a magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) machine, comprising: a housing configured to couple to a head end of the MRI machine; a fan integrated into the housing; a plurality of vents disposed to circulate air blown from the fan around a patient lying in the MRI machine; and an inlet for receipt of an air source, the inlet in fluid connection with the fan. 2. The blower system of claim 1 , wherein the housing is removable from the MRI machine. 3. The blower system of claim 1, wherein the housing is integrated with the MRI machine. 4. The blower system of claim 1, further comprising a central bore in the housing, wherein the vents are arranged around the central bore. 5. The blower system of claim 4, further comprising a sloped inner surface defining the central bore. 6. The blower system of claim 1, further comprising a wired remote control connected to a controller in the housing. 7. The blower system of claim 1 , further comprising an oxygen source in fluid communication with the fan. 8. The blower system of claim 7, further comprising an oxygen sensor and a controller configured to actuate the oxygen source in response to the oxygen sensor detecting a loss of oxygen above a threshold level. 9. The blower system of claim 7, wherein the oxygen source is located in a first room and the MRI machine is located in a second room. 10. The blower system of claim 1, further comprising a helium sensor and a controller configured to actuate the fan in response to the helium sensor detecting helium above a threshold level.
